description: SecretValue::new(bytes: Vec<u8>) -> Self, SecretValue::as_bytes() -> &[u8], and SecretValue::into_bytes() -> Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> in secretx-core/src/lib.rs have no doc comments or only minimal ones. These are core API entry points used by every backend and every caller. Missing documentation means: (1) no contract statement for new() about ownership transfer and zeroization guarantee, (2) no contract for as_bytes() about whether it returns the full value or a trimmed/decoded form, (3) no contract for into_bytes() about what Zeroizing guarantees (zeroed on drop, but not on reallocation within the Vec). A caller who reads only the type signature of new() does not know that passing bytes containing a trailing newline is their responsibility to strip. Fix: add /// doc comments to all three methods stating the contract clearly.
title: SecretValue::new and related methods lack doc comments
